_comment: ops-7 (#430) — pinned SHA256 + size for downloaded model/wheel artifacts. install-kokoro.ps1 (PowerShell Get-FileHash) and install-qwen3.mjs (Node crypto) verify a download against these and refuse + delete on mismatch. The Model Manager inventory reflects a lightweight on-disk SIZE match as the `integrity` badge. RE-BLESS when an upstream asset legitimately changes: download the new asset, compute its SHA256 (PowerShell `Get-FileHash -Algorithm SHA256 <file>` / `sha256sum <file>`), and update the entry — otherwise a stale pin reads as a false mismatch and blocks install.

flashAttentionWheel._comment: Single-maintainer community FA2 wheel — the sharpest supply-chain risk (runs with the user's privileges on install). Opt-in (Windows AMD64 + cp311 only). UNPINNED until blessed on a box that has the wheel: download it, sha256 it, and set `sha256` here. Until then install-qwen3.mjs warns + proceeds (it can't verify without a pin).
